description: Medical device giant Stryker is grappling with a cyberattack that has disrupted its global network, with an Iranian-linked group claiming responsibility. While services remain impacted, the company is prioritizing customer-facing systems like ordering and shipping.

description: A 17-year-old girl was allegedly kidnapped, raped, and tied to a pole overnight in Beed district. Police arrested her 29-year-old former boyfriend based on her statement. The incident highlights a disturbing act of violence and the subsequent legal proceedings initiated by the authorities.

Red Sea alternative to Strait of Hormuz? Saudi's offer to oil buyers amid supply crunch
description: Saudi Arabia is offering long-term oil buyers a new route via its Red Sea port of Yanbu, bypassing the disrupted Strait of Hormuz. This move aims to ensure supply continuity for April shipments, though pipeline capacity limits full allocations. Asian buyers, including China's Sinopec, are already adjusting refinery operations due to supply concerns, highlighting global uncertainty over the conflict's duration.

title: India-US trade deal: Commerce secy says ‘actual signing when new architecture in place’
description: The comments assume significance at a time when the US Supreme Court has struck down the tariffs that were imposed by the Trump administration, calling them illegal.

title: Trade deficit for Feb widens to $3.96 bn: India’s exports climb 11%, imports surge 21%
description: India's exports reached $76.13 billion in February 2026, showing strong growth. Imports rose even faster, leading to a wider trade deficit. For the fiscal year, exports are on track for growth. The country previously set a record in exports for the 2024-25 fiscal year. Challenges like the West Asia crisis may impact March exports.

title: Russia-Ukraine clashes: Explosions rock Kyiv in rare daytime attack
description: Kyiv faced a rare daytime Russian assault Monday as air defenses intercepted projectiles, with debris falling in the city center. This escalation follows Moscow's claim of repelling a major Ukrainian drone attack over the weekend. Meanwhile, US peace efforts are reportedly stalled amid the widening Middle East conflict, highlighting the growing role of drones in modern warfare.

title: Did PM Modi know about US-Israel's Iran strike plan? What Israeli envoy said
description: Israel's envoy, Reuven Azar, clarified that the military operation against Iran was timed post-PM Modi's visit to seize an "operational opportunity." He emphasized Israel's anticipation of Iranian retaliation and its commitment to neutralizing nuclear and missile threats, while also debunking disinformation about PM Netanyahu. Israel aims to prevent the Iranian regime from reconstituting its plans.

Watch: Fire erupts again at Fujairah port in UAE, harbour suspends oil loadings
description: Fujairah port in the UAE experienced a drone attack, igniting a fire at a petroleum facility. While emergency teams quickly contained the blaze with no reported injuries, oil loadings were suspended. This marks a recent escalation in attacks on the vital oil hub, underscoring regional tensions and the port's critical role in global energy trade.

The incident occurred when his bike collided with a scooter that suddenly appeared, causing him to lose control. The accident was captured on camera, and police are investigating.

title: Roger Federer image questioned as insider says Rafael Nadal felt more authentic
description: A Roland Garros insider, Gilles Jourdan, recently claimed Rafael Nadal appeared more authentic and approachable than Roger Federer during their careers. Speaking to L’Équipe, Jourdan said Federer seemed focused on business and money, while Nadal built stronger relationships at the tournament. Despite the criticism, Federer remains one of tennis’ greatest champions and a billionaire through endorsements and investments.

title: ‘Mama’ in the spotlight: Himanta Sarma emerges as Assam's most loved and loathed leader
description: Assam's Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma, a former Congress rebel, is a polarizing figure, lauded for rebranding the state as a growth hub and combating infiltrators, while criticized for his combative rhetoric. Known affectionately as 'mama' by youth, Sarma's political acumen and social media presence are central to his strategy, even as he faces accusations of hate speech.

title: Why BMC removed red-blue beacon from Mumbai mayor Ritu Tawde’s car
description: Mumbai's Mayor Ritu Tawde, Deputy Mayor Sanjay Ghadi, and Leader of the House Ganesh Khankar had red-and-blue beacons removed from their official vehicles following a controversy. The BMC administration's lapse in providing these unauthorized lights led to criticism from the opposition and RTI activists, despite the Chief Minister defending the mayor.

title: FASTag annual pass gets pricier: Here's what changes from April 1
description: The National Highways Authority of India is increasing the FASTag Annual Pass fee. Starting April 1, 2026, the pass will cost Rs 3,075. This prepaid toll plan is for non-commercial private vehicles. The revision aligns with National Highways Fee Rules. Over 56 lakh users have adopted the pass. The new rate applies to eligible vehicles at approximately 1,150 fee plazas.

title: NFL trade rumors: Jordan Love’s Green Bay Packers linked to $321m ex-Minnesota Vikings QB
description: ESPN’s Bill Barnwell suggests Green Bay Packers as the perfect landing spot for veteran quarterback Kirk Cousins. With Jordan Love’s seldom absences, Cousins could serve as a trusted backup or bridge starter. His extensive experience and constant motivation to play at a high level make him a valuable gem for Green Bay. If he wants a Super Bowl before retirement, then the Packers’ the possible place.

title: $3.4 trillion mistake with China: Why US will make India's rise far tougher
description: The US has spent a staggering $3.4 trillion on a "militarised rivalry" with China since 2012, a figure dwarfing its Afghanistan war costs. This massive peacetime spending, driven by a strategic pivot to Asia, highlights Washington's determination to avoid repeating past "mistakes" with Beijing, particularly concerning technological transfers and economic engagement, as it now recalibrates its approach with India.
